raw_recent_text: "Ugh, not again," you spout as the old wagon shutters, then dies.
Stranded on the road again, but this time, your money has gone dry.
All but your 50 dollar bill that Grandma gave you on her last Christmas.
You've been saving it for when you needed it most, but surely, it won't be enough.
The door creaks open, and you step out into the night sky, coughing up the smoke from your fried vehicle.
After pushing your car off the road and between the trees, there isn't much else left for you to do,
so you begin to wander down the dark, lonely street.
But at the end of the road, where concrete turned to stone turned to dirt, you notice a light up ahead, on the top of a hill.
As you waltz into the old, wooden shack, your eyes begin to light up with the fire of a thousand suns.
Roulette wheels! Poker tables! And in a dark corner of the abandoned casino, sits a dealer, shuffling cards for a new round of Blackjack.
That 50 dollars might just come in handy after all. Thanks, Grandma!
As you go to sit down at the table, you hear the Dealer cough, then watch as he sits up.
In a deep, and yet strained voice, the Dealer, cloaked in darkness, poses a question to you.
Would you like to play a game of Blackjack?
You have $50

raw_recent_text: Without questioning his word, and with your winnings in hand, you scurry to the door, eager to get some sleep after such a long day.
Making it back to your car, ditched on the side of the road, but no longer engulfed in smoke, you lay down, and close your eyes. It's time to rest.
~ ~ ~ Day Summary ~ ~ ~
Excellent!
You've survived 1 day!
You started your journey with just $50.
Since then, you've accumulated $10.
That brings you to a grand total of $60!
Let's not get too far ahead of ourselves though, you're still quite poor.
I like your work ethic. Your grandma would be proud.
Don't forget what the bible taught you:
"Remember, it's not paranoia if your plants are actually plotting against you. Keep them in check by pretending to water them with vinegar."
Press a key to continue: ~ ~ ~ Morning, Day 2 ~ ~ ~  True
Your body feels like it got a factory reset overnight. Joints? Smooth. Back? Fine. Mood? Immaculate.
A scraggly stray cat jumps onto your hood and stares at you through the windshield.
It meows. Loudly. Insistently. Like it's judging your entire existence.
"Remember, it's not paranoia if your plants are actually plotting against you. Keep them in check by pretending to water them with vinegar."
Press a key to continue: ~ ~ ~ Morning, Day 2 ~ ~ ~  True
Your body feels like it got a factory reset overnight. Joints? Smooth. Back? Fine. Mood? Immaculate.
A scraggly stray cat jumps onto your hood and stares at you through the windshield.
It meows. Loudly. Insistently. Like it's judging your entire existence.
You share your meager rations. The cat purrs and curls up on your hood.
It's still there when you wake up the next morning. You have a friend now.
You decide to call it Whiskers.
You have befriended Whiskers!
Whiskers drops a dead bug on your chest to wake you up. Breakfast in bed, cat-style.
Having Whiskers by your side makes the world a little less cruel.
You hear scratching at your window. It's your cat friend!
The scraggly stray has returned, meowing insistently.
You open the window a crack. It pushes its head through and purrs.
You're not alone. Not entirely.
As the sun begins to set, and the stars light up in the night sky, you walk to the casino, eager to play more Blackjack.
The green felt remembers you. So do I.

You wake up refreshed and optimistic. The optimism will probably wear off by noon, but for now, you bask in it.
Your car breaks down on a stretch of road with no cell service.
The sun beats down. You're stuck.
You sit on the hood and wait. And wait. And wait.
Three hours later, someone finally stops.
They charge you $25 for the ride. At least you're not dead.
Your new balance is $59 - $25 = $34
Whiskers demands breakfast by walking across your face. Repeatedly.
Having Whiskers by your side makes the world a little less cruel.
The cat is lying next to your car. Not moving.
You know before you touch it. You know. But you touch it anyway.
Cold. Stiff. Gone.
You bury it behind a gas station. Mark the spot with a rock.
It was just a stray. Just a cat. But it was YOUR cat. And now it's gone.
Your mind feels... foggy. Something isn't right.
As the sun begins to set, and the stars light up in the night sky, you walk to the casino, eager to play more Blackjack.
You feel anxious tonight.
Night falls. The game begins.

Your current sanity: 73%
You actually feel rested. Like, genuinely rested. This is what sleeping is supposed to feel like?
Whiskers sits in a sunbeam, ignoring your existence with practiced elegance.
The itching is maddening. Especially at night. Tiny burrows appearing between your fingers, on your wrists, in your armpits.
Microscopic mites have burrowed into your skin. They're laying eggs under your flesh.
You scratch until you bleed, but the relief is only momentary.
Scabies. Your skin is infested.
As the sun begins to set, and the stars light up in the night sky, you walk to the casino, eager to play more Blackjack.
You feel anxious tonight.
Welcome, welcome. Have a seat, and we can begin.
Whiskers is already awake, sitting perfectly still on the dashboard, silhouetted against the sunrise. She looks regal.
Having Whiskers by your side makes the world a little less cruel.
You can't sleep. You decide to take a walk.
The city is different at night. Quieter. More honest, somehow. The neon signs hum a frequency only insomniacs can hear.
You walk past closed shops and sleeping houses. Everyone behind those walls has a bed. A fridge. A door that locks from the inside.
You walk until your legs get tired, then you walk some more. The rhythm helps. Step, step, step. Simple. Forward. One direction.
When you get back to your car, you feel calmer. Tired in the right way.
As the sun begins to set, and the stars light up in the night sky, you walk to the casino, eager to play more Blackjack.
You keep coming back. Interesting.

you can't sleep. you decide to take a walk.

you walk past closed shops and sleeping houses. everyone behind those walls has a bed. a fridge. a door that locks from the inside.
you walk until your legs get tired, then you walk some more. the rhythm helps. step, step, step. simple. forward. one direction.
when you get back to your car, you feel calmer. tired in the right way.

Press a key to continue: ~ ~ ~ Morning, Day 5 ~ ~ ~  True
You slept so well that you're suspicious. Something this good can't be free.
You drive your car to a gas station for supplies. Just snacks. Maybe some coffee.
You're browsing the chips when the door slams open.
"EVERYBODY ON THE GROUND! NOW!"
A man with a shotgun. Ski mask. Shaking hands. This is really happening.
The cashier freezes. A mother clutches her child. An old man drops his coffee.
The robber swings the gun around, wild-eyed.
A man with a shotgun. Ski mask. Shaking hands. This is really happening.
The cashier freezes. A mother clutches her child. An old man drops his coffee.
The robber swings the gun around, wild-eyed.
You duck behind a shelf. Slowly, carefully, you crawl toward the back.
The robber is focused on the register. You're almost to the storage room...
You make it. Hide behind boxes of toilet paper and canned goods.
You can hear everything. The shouting. The crying. The crash of the register hitting the floor.
Then sirens. Running footsteps. Silence.
The police find you an hour later, still hiding. Curled up like a child.
Your mind feels... foggy. Something isn't right.
Whiskers is grooming excessively. Stress cleaning. She's not okay.
As you're walking along the side of the road, something catches your eye. A flash of white, darting between the bushes. A rabbit!
Without thinking, you give chase. The little creature bounds ahead of you, zigging and zagging through the underbrush with seemingly effortless grace.
You run and run, but no matter how fast you go, the rabbit stays just out of reach. Its white tail bobs mockingly in the moonlight.
Finally, you stop, hands on your knees, gasping for breath. When you look up, the rabbit is gone, vanished into the night like it was never there.
You trudge back to your wagon, defeated. But something tells you this isn't the last time you'll see that rabbit.
As the sun begins to set, and the stars light up in the night sky, you walk to the casino, eager to play more Blackjack.
You feel anxious tonight.
Shuffle. Deal. Win. Lose. Repeat. Shall we?

Your current sanity: 62%
A bird is singing outside your car. You don't want to punch it. Progress.
You stop by the convenience store for your usual run. The clerk behind the counter —
you've seen him before, skinny kid, maybe 19, name tag says KYLE — looks rough today.
His eyes are red. His hands are shaking when he scans your items. He drops your change twice.
"Sorry. Sorry about that. Long night."
He tries to smile but it comes out wrong. Like he forgot how smiles work.
Kyle blinks at you, surprised. Like nobody's asked him that in a while.
"I'm... yeah. I'm fine. It's just... never mind. Thanks for asking though. Seriously."
He gives you a real smile this time. Small, but real.
You grab your bag and head out. Something tells you Kyle is not, in fact, fine.
Whiskers sits with her back to you. The cold shoulder is real.
The dream is simple this time. You're just... not worried. About anything.
No Dealer. No casino. No car. Just a warm room and the knowledge that tomorrow will be okay.
That's the dream. Not a million dollars. Just okay.
You wake up and the okay evaporates. But you remember what it felt like.
As the sun begins to set, and the stars light up in the night sky, you walk to the casino, eager to play more Blackjack.
You feel anxious tonight.
Each night you return. Each night, the cards wait.

As the sun begins to set, and the stars light up in the night sky, you walk to the casino, eager to play more Blackjack.
You feel anxious tonight.
Still alive? Good. The cards would be disappointed otherwise.
Whiskers won't eat. She just stares at the food, then at you, then turns away.
Sitting in your car, your mind drifts. You dream of water. Dark water. Deep water. Rising water.
You're in a car—your car—and water is pouring in through every crack.
You can't open the doors. The pressure is too much. The windows won't break.
The water reaches your waist. Your chest. Your neck. Your mouth.
You scream but water fills your lungs instead of air.
You wake up GASPING. Clawing at your throat. Soaking wet with sweat.
Just a dream. Just a dream. Just a—
Your feet are wet. You look down.
Rain. It's raining. Water leaked through a crack in your window.
Just rain. You're fine. You're fine. You're fine.
The edges of your vision blur. Reality feels thin.
